extraction.one_line_summary: KITTEL Marcel won the sprint, but the intermediate sprint saw CARUSO steal 1 second back from KWIATKOWSKI, exploiting Sky's positioning failure.
tactical_patterns: Sky allowed rival teams to contest intermediate sprint unchallenged, enabling Caruso to claw back GC time through bonus seconds.
EF Education launched long-range leadout with Modolo but surrendered to Quick-Step's controlled pace, revealing sprint train dominance hierarchy.
Sagan positioned only two wheels from Kittel with 1.4km remaining but failed to contest, signaling form deficit in power finishes.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: negative: suffered second mechanical of the race at 17.7km, continuing pattern of equipment misfortune affecting GC defense
rider_signals.CARUSO Damiano: positive: tactically alert to exploit intermediate sprint when Sky failed to cover, actively stealing back GC seconds
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: negative: lost hard-won GC time at intermediate sprint through poor team positioning, Sky failed to protect his advantage
rider_signals.KITTEL Marcel: positive: secured second stage win despite Ackermann's late challenge, showing recovered form after career struggles
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: positioned perfectly 1.4km out but didn't contest sprint, either strategic or form-related weakness in flat power finishes
rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: nearly overtook Kittel in final meters despite inferior leadout support, confirming top-tier sprint speed
